subject: Statistics
title: Bayesian Credible Intervals
description: Learn Bayesian Credible Intervals in one minute.

hook.text: A 95% credible interval actually means what everyone thinks a 95% confidence interval means.

buildup.text: Bayesian credible intervals say: there's a 95% probability the parameter is inside this range.

discovery.text: This direct probability statement requires a prior — your belief before seeing data. Frequentists avoid priors.

twist.text: With enough data, credible and confidence intervals converge. The philosophical difference fades with sample size.

climax.text: The credible interval is more intuitive but requires more assumptions. Neither approach is universally better.

punchline.text: It means what you always wished confidence intervals meant.

quiz.question: How does a Bayesian credible interval differ from a confidence interval?
options: It gives a direct probability that the parameter is in the range — but requires a prior
It's always wider than a confidence interval
